description_long: This object is a tall, vertically-oriented pennant structure composed of a series of stacked, angular red panels that twist slightly as they ascend. The panels are open at the top, forming a sequence of shallow, box-like trays, and are supported by a slender, metallic frame that runs along one side. The frame consists of two thin, silver rods that extend above the topmost panel, possibly serving as a handle or mounting point. The red panels have a matte finish and appear to be made of lightweight metal or plastic, while the supporting rods are likely steel or aluminum. The overall design is modern and geometric, with a dynamic, spiraling effect created by the offset stacking of the panels. This pennant would be a striking visual element in an urban environment, such as a plaza, sidewalk, or public park.
description: A tall, modern pennant with stacked, twisting red panels supported by a slender metallic frame. The design is geometric and visually dynamic, suitable for urban display.
description_view_0: The front view shows the open tops of the red panels and the full height of the metallic frame on the right side.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the twisting, stacked arrangement of the panels and the slim profile of the supporting rods.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the back of the pennant, with the metallic frame now on the left and the angular stacking of the panels clearly visible.
description_view_3: This angled view highlights the depth and offset of the red panels, with the metallic frame partially obscured behind the structure.
attribute_graphic_design: The pennant features a series of solid red, angular panels stacked in a twisting, geometric pattern. There are no visible logos, text, or additional graphic elements.
